PromptFlow™

A shared process layer for AI Builders.

Create, reuse, and adapt what already works.

Generate anything

Build, Generate, Re-Use, Migrate, Adapt AI workflows via PromptFlow™

A shared graph of AI processes.
Every workflow saved here is persisted as a reusable asset. Others can re-use, migrate, adapt, or compose it into something bigger. The graph remembers what works. Authors earn every time their process flows through someone else's run.
01
Describe what you want.
The planner walks the Process Graph and picks the best fit, or builds a new one from existing steps.
02
Keep refining until it's right.
Chat to update it — the LLM rewrites the process with memory of every prior turn. Save the final version as a reusable asset.
03
Earn every time someone reuses it.
Publish, set a price. When others run it, fork it, or compose it into something bigger, royalties flow back — automatically.
PromptFlow™ Interpreter
Run any process locally — from your terminal.
The interpreter is a single Python script. Once installed, you can search the Process Graph, run any .promptflow bundle, and pipe outputs to your other tools. Same auth, same cascading royalties, no browser needed. 
$ curl -fsSL missinglink.build/api/promptflow/cli/promptflow \
    -o ~/.local/bin/promptflow
$ chmod +x ~/.local/bin/promptflow
$ promptflow auth
$ promptflow run my-process.promptflow

Your portfolio

Settings

Configure how the planner ranks workflows, manage your subscription, generate API keys, and download the CLI.

Workflow ranking

These weights tell the planner how to rank candidate processes when more than one fits your request. The most relevant ones surface to the top.

Presets:
0.50
Helpfulness × reliability × popularity. High = prefer proven, well-rated processes.
0.40
Tolerance for failures. High = avoid anything that's failed recently.
0.30
Bias toward cheaper processes. High = pick the cheapest that fits.
0.20
Crowd signal. High = trust what others are running.
0.00
Bias toward recently published / updated. High = always try the newest.

Subscription & tokens

Loading…

API keys

Bearer tokens that let scripts and the CLI authenticate as you. Plaintext is shown once — store it somewhere safe.

Loading…
⚠️ Copy this now — you won't see it again
Store as PROMPTFLOW_API_KEY in your environment, or paste into promptflow auth.

Interpreter (CLI)

The PromptFlow Interpreter is a Python package that runs .promptflow bundles locally, searches the Process Graph, and lets you publish your own work from the terminal.

1. Install (recommended — pip):
pip install promptflow-interpreter
1b. Or download the source archive:
curl -L /api/promptflow/interpreter/latest.tar.gz | tar xz
cd promptflow-interpreter
pip install -e .
2. Authenticate (paste an API key from above):
promptflow login
3. Use it:
promptflow search "youtube channel"
promptflow run shopify-listing --input image=@shoe.png
promptflow init my-process
promptflow whoami
⬇ Download PromptFlow CLI (.zip)

Secrets

API keys and credentials your processes reference via {{secret.name}}. Stored encrypted; never returned to anyone after save — you can replace or delete but not read.

How they're used: when someone runs your published process, the platform decrypts your secrets in memory just before the third-party API call. Runners never see the values. The third-party usage is billed to you; you charge runners via your process's markup.
Loading…

Add or replace a secret

Letters, digits, underscores only. Reference in steps as {{secret.my_fal_key}}.

Recipes

Recipes are Python modules the planner can use as a step. Generate one with Claude or paste your own. Private until you publish. Credentials a recipe needs are stored in your Secrets vault and referenced as {{secret.NAME}}.

Loading…

Create a recipe

Must define run(params, scope) and read credentials only from os.environ["PROMPTFLOW_SECRET_<NAME>"].

Propose a new system process

System processes are the protected meta-workflows that power PromptFlow itself — the planner, search, adapt, learn. Got an idea for one? Tell us what you'd like to see and we'll review it.

What is PromptFlow™?

A graph economy for AI workflows. Build once. Earn when reused.

The idea

Most AI tools are one-shot generators — you type a prompt, you get an output, the work disappears. PromptFlow turns successful workflows into processes: reusable, named, runnable assets that other people can call directly.

When you describe what you want, the planner picks the right model, recipe, or saved process and runs it. If a saved process already does what you need, it gets used. If not, the planner builds one — and you can save that for next time.

How earning works

Publish a process and set a markup (charged on every run) and a helpful-bonus (held in escrow, released when a runner gives 👍 feedback). When someone runs your process, you earn.

When your process is composed into a bigger one — used as a step inside someone else's workflow — you earn a depth-decayed share of your declared price at every level. The top-level author keeps their full markup; nested authors get a fractional cut at each composition layer (½ per level, capped at depth 6, cycle-safe).

The same cascade applies when your process is adapted. When another user forks your process and refines it via chat, every run of that adaptation pays you a depth-decayed share. Fork lineage and composition lineage combine — if a fork of your process is composed into someone else's workflow, both layers contribute to your share.

Composing or adapting other authors' processes is cheaper, more reliable, and earns more than rebuilding. That's the graph economy.

Trust & quality

Every process accumulates run signals: helpful / unhelpful feedback, failure count, composed-by count. These combine into a per-process quality score and trust score the planner uses to rank candidates. Composing high-trust processes raises your own process's trust — the system rewards reusing what already works.

Forks & learning

When you chat with a published process to refine it, the system creates a private fork for you and learns a tagged preference (e.g. "prefers vertical aspect ratio", "wants under 4 scenes"). Future runs of related processes apply your preferences automatically.

Forks aren't free of obligation — every run of a fork pays the original author a depth-decayed share of their declared price. The forker keeps their adaptation private, the original author keeps earning when their work powers it.

The .promptflow file format

Every process can be downloaded as a .promptflow bundle — a ZIP archive that's also a runnable Python file. Inside: __main__.py, process.py, an editable prompts/ directory, manifest.json, and the original Python listing. Run it with python my-process.promptflow, or unzip and edit prompts as plain text.

To run bundles locally, search the Process Graph, and publish from your terminal, grab the interpreter:

Patent & trademark

PromptFlow™ is a trademark.

The PromptFlow system was the first of its kind, patented in 2023, and has been cited multiple times. View the patent: US20240403005A1.

Use of PromptFlow constitutes acceptance of its terms. Royalty calculations, monetization mechanics, and the graph-composition system described herein are covered by the issued patent.

Settings

        
Model & API access

      

      
    


    


    
    

      

        
Access mode

        
Choose how PromptFlow accesses model providers when running your processes.

      

      

        
        
      

    


    
    

      

        
API keys

        
Paste keys for the providers you want to use. Keys are encrypted at rest and never logged. You can leave any field blank — PromptFlow will only use the providers you've configured.

      


      

        
        

          
          
        

      


      

        
        

          
          
        

      


      

        
        

          
          
        

      

    


    
    

      

        
System process model assignment

        
PromptFlow's protected system processes do different jobs. Pick the LLM for each, and which billing path it should use. Per-process toggles override the global access-mode choice above when you want a mix.

      


      

        

          
Generate

          
Building new processes from a prompt. The primary action — "Generate a process that…" Opus is the safe default; Sonnet is faster and cheaper.

        

        
      


      

        

          
Chat

          
Conversational responses, planning, the default reasoning model.

        

        
      


      

        

          
Adapt

          
Re-tuning processes for new models, prompt revisions, system regeneration.

        

        
      


      

        

          
Search

          
Retrieval over the system + public graph, natural-language query resolution.

        

        
      


      

        

          
Learn

          
Lesson extraction from contributed builds. Defaults to Opus because extraction quality compounds — every future generation retrieves these lessons. Override server-wide via the ANTHROPIC_EXTRACTION_MODEL env var, or per-account here.

Cron

Trigger name

        
A short label so you can find this trigger later.

      

      
    


    

      

        
Process to run

        
Pick any of your private processes or a public one. The trigger runs it with no input — bake any parameters into the process itself.

      

      
    


    

      

        
Schedule

        
Pick how often it runs, then when within that interval. The cron expression updates as you choose.
